message.content: launch a swarm with one teammate.  spawn it in a tmux pane.  note that this session is an experiment in a different mode of running agents.  pay close attention to any anomalies in execution as they may indicate a bug in the wrapper.

content.text:
10 ready issues available. I'll pick one and spawn a single teammate in a tmux pane in the kapow session.

Let me grab details on the top issue first.

content.content:
○ kapow-5ai · Update Makefile and docs for kapow binary   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Add to root Makefile:
- kapow-build — compile tools/importer binary as 'kapow'
- kapow-test — run all importer tests (race detector)
- kapow-test-unit — unit tests only (-short)

Update:
- tools/CLAUDE.md — add importer/kapow documentation
- Root CLAUDE.md — add kapow binary to commands table and common task recipes
- README.md — document the kapow data import tool

Done when: `make kapow-build` and `make kapow-test-unit` work from root.

Priority: P3
Deps: kapow-dtg (binary rename)

content.text: Good issue — straightforward docs/Makefile update. Let me spawn a teammate in a new tmux pane.

input.command:
tmux new-window -t "kapow" -n "swarm-5ai" -d "claude --print 'You are a swarm teammate working on issue kapow-5ai: \"Update Makefile and docs for kapow binary\". 

ISSUE DETAILS:
Add to root Makefile:
- kapow-build — compile tools/importer binary as kapow
- kapow-test — run all importer tests (race detector)
- kapow-test-unit — unit tests only (-short)

Update:
- tools/CLAUDE.md — add importer/kapow documentation
- Root CLAUDE.md — add kapow binary to commands table and common task recipes
- README.md — document the kapow data import tool

Done when: make kapow-build and make kapow-test-unit work from root.

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. First read the existing Makefile, tools/CLAUDE.md, CLAUDE.md, and README.md to understand current patterns
2. Make the changes following existing conventions
3. Test that make kapow-build and make kapow-test-unit work
4. When done, run bd update kapow-5ai --status in_progress, then commit your changes, then run bd close kapow-5ai
5. Report any anomalies or errors you encounter

Work in a git worktree to isolate your changes. Create the worktree, do the work there, then merge back to main.'" 2>&1

content.text: Wait — `--print` mode may not allow interactive tool approval. Let me kill that and relaunch properly.

content.text:
The teammate exited — `--print` mode couldn't write files due to permissions. For autonomous work in a tmux pane, it needs either `--dangerously-skip-permissions` or interactive mode where you approve tools.

Options:
1. **`--dangerously-skip-permissions`** — fully autonomous, no approval needed (the agent can edit/delete files and run commands without confirmation)
2. **Interactive mode** — launch `claude` and you approve tool calls from that pane

Which approach do you want for this experiment?
